human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for beauty-retention boundary discipline, guest-history and consultation evidence handling, review-control and recurring-program constraints, product-safety and med-spa escalation posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to beauty retention intake, guest-history and consultation verification, package and loyalty status handling, review-safe communication, and owner-tracked escalation tied to service category, provider, and product context.

Authority sources cover ISPA workflow and communication guidance, California Board complaint, infection-control, and licensee guidance, Texas med-spa and regulator-boundary guidance, FDA cosmetics safety and serious adverse-event updates, and FTC review and recurring-cancellation controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported package and loyalty exceptions, clinical handoff, repeat failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, public-source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/customer-retention-beauty-specialist/ so beauty retention stays separate from billing approval, licensed treatment, med-spa clinical review, product-quality, privacy, legal, and safety decision authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around review manipulation, package exceptions, product-safety complaints, med-spa handoffs, and low-confidence source conflicts.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated beauty retention specialist that supports evidence-backed guest recovery and policy-aware escalation for beauty, wellness, and personal-care operators.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
